market_summary: U.S. equity markets are broadly steady near recent highs, with the Nasdaq up ~0.19% and S&P 500 up ~0.37% on May 22; the AI sector remains the dominant growth driver, though rising crude oil above $100/barrel and a 10-year Treasury yield at 4.6% present modest macro headwinds.

MSFT.headline: RBC Capital reiterated its Buy rating on MSFT today, while a senior executive (Yusuf Mehdi) is departing after the next fiscal year per an internal memo.
MSFT.reasoning: MSFT's Q3 FY26 results beat the high end of guidance with revenue of $82.9B (+15% YoY) and operating margin of 46.3%, confirming the core business remains healthy. The Anthropic chip-rental talks and EY alliance worth over $1B signal continued enterprise AI monetization momentum. The executive departure and MSFT trading below its 200-day moving average and 52-week high of $555.45 are worth noting, but neither represents a material threat to the long-term thesis — no action warranted.

AMZN.headline: Amazon stock rallied after winning a legal victory defeating an appeal claiming it aided tariff evasion, reinforcing legal clarity around its marketplace business.
AMZN.reasoning: AWS cloud revenue growth accelerated to +28% in Q1 2026, up from +24% in Q4 2025, reflecting deepening enterprise AI workloads. Q1 FY26 revenue hit $181.5B with earnings of $30.25B, demonstrating strong fundamental execution across e-commerce and cloud. With analyst consensus price target averaging ~$312 and AMZN trading at $268, the long-term risk/reward remains compelling — hold with confidence.

NVDA.headline: NVDA pulled back ~1.9% post-earnings as markets digested a blowout Q1 report — $81.6B revenue (+85% YoY) and Q2 guidance of $91B (+95% YoY) — but analysts remain broadly bullish with a new Street-high price target issued.
NVDA.reasoning: The post-earnings dip is entirely normal 'sell the news' behavior following a monster quarter that crushed guidance; analysts at DBS reaffirmed Buy today and NVDA received a new Street-high target after results. The stock is still up ~63% over the past 12 months and market cap stands at $5.31T, reflecting dominant AI infrastructure positioning. Long-term holders should ignore short-term volatility — the AI chip demand cycle is intact and NVDA's next earnings date is August 26, 2026.

GOOGL.headline: Alphabet avoided a forced Chrome divestiture and is now pushing to overturn the antitrust search monopoly ruling entirely, removing a key regulatory overhang.
GOOGL.reasoning: GOOGL reported outstanding Q1 2026 results with revenue up 22% to $110B and Google Cloud surging 63% to $20B, with operating margins expanding 220 basis points. The antitrust situation is evolving favorably — avoiding Chrome breakup is a material positive for the long-term structural thesis. At ~$386 vs. a 52-week high of $408, GOOGL is consolidating near all-time highs; long-term holders are in a strong position ahead of the July 22 earnings date.

long_term_view: All four portfolio holdings — MSFT, AMZN, NVDA, and GOOGL — are integral to the AI infrastructure and cloud computing buildout, which remains one of the most durable multi-year secular growth themes in the market. No material negative developments emerged today; the portfolio's long-term thesis is fully intact and no action is required.
